name: MAI-Code-1-Flash
summary: Microsoft's first homegrown coding model lands inside GitHub Copilot for individual users — turn on auto-pick or grab it from the model picker in VS Code, no setup needed; it beats Claude Haiku 4.5 on coding benchmarks while burning up to 60% fewer tokens, and dials reasoning up or down per request so simple edits stay snappy; one of seven new MAI models (plus a reasoning model and a voice cloner) Microsoft shipped at Build to cut its OpenAI bill and pass the savings to devs

name: Rodeo
summary: video-understanding startup TwelveLabs takes its tech straight to creators — describe the shot you want in plain English and Rodeo searches your entire footage library, finds the clips, and assembles a rough cut for you; powered by their Marengo 3.0 + Pegasus 1.5 models, it turns 'I know the clip is in here somewhere' into an instant edit instead of an afternoon of scrubbing timelines

name: Claude Opus 4.8
summary: Anthropic's new flagship lands today on claude.ai, Claude Code, the API, and Cowork — tops GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic coding benchmarks and is ~4x less likely than 4.7 to leave flaws in code it writes; same price as 4.7 ($5/M in, $25/M out) but fast mode is now 3x cheaper, plus a research preview of dynamic workflows that spawns hundreds of parallel subagents for jobs too big for one context window

name: Gemini Spark
summary: Google's 24/7 personal AI agent rolls into beta — runs on a cloud VM that keeps working when your laptop is closed, schedules and chains multi-step tasks across Gmail/Docs, and uses MCP to act inside Canva, OpenTable, and Instacart at launch (Adobe, Spotify, GitHub, Notion, and Slack queued for summer); opens this week for U.S. Google AI Ultra subscribers

name: Emdash
summary: open-source desktop app for running coding agents in parallel — each one isolated in its own git worktree; supports 22 CLI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, Amp + 16 more), pulls tickets from Linear/Jira/GitHub Issues, and ships with a built-in Kanban board to track agent progress across tasks

name: DeepSeek V4-Pro Price Cut
summary: DeepSeek made its 75%-off promo the permanent list price — V4-Pro now runs $0.44/M in and $0.87/M out, roughly 17x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7 on output and ~34x cheaper than GPT-5.5; the model is MIT-licensed with a 1M-token context, so a solo builder can run frontier-tier coding through the API (or self-host) for pocket change
